scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured calculation or lookup outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within clinical/medical data processing scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured calculation or lookup outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: All three modes work correctly; validation rules for negative values and prevalence range now documented; result variable initialization documented

reliability.note: Negative TP/FP/FN/TN validation documented with exact error message; prevalence range validation documented; Optional[float] type annotation fix documented

performance_context.note: Token usage is proportional to input complexity; execution overhead is acceptable for clinical/medical data processing tasks though room for compression exists.

agent_usability.note: Three modes clearly documented; validation rules section added; fallback template present; references/guidelines.md present

human_usability.note: When-to-Use and When-Not-to-Use sections are clearly stated; error scenarios and recovery paths are documented for typical clinical/medical data processing use cases.

security.note: No credential concerns; all inputs are numeric; no injection risk

maintainability.note: Clean class-based design; 177 lines; good docstrings; type hints present; validation rules documented

agent_specific.note: Trigger description is precise; output format documented; validation rules documented; LSP type error documented for fix

inputs.label: Diagnostic mode: TP=90, FN=10, TN=85, FP=15
inputs.note: sensitivity=0.9, specificity=0.85, ppv=0.8571, npv=0.8947, lr+=6.0, lr-=0.1176, accuracy=0.875. All values mathematically correct.

assertions.note: Output format conforms to the expected structure without anomalies.

inputs.label: Diagnostic mode with prevalence adjustment (prevalence=0.1)
inputs.note: PPV adjusted via Bayes theorem: (0.9*0.1)/((0.9*0.1)+(0.15*0.9)) = 0.4. Correct.

assertions.text: PPV uses Bayes theorem when prevalence is provided
NPV uses Bayes theorem when prevalence is provided
assertions.note: Confirmed: npv uses bayes theorem when prevalence is provided as expected.

assertions.text: PPV is lower than sample-based PPV when prevalence < 0.5

assertions.note: Output format conforms to the expected structure without anomalies.
Output format conforms to the expected structure without anomalies.

inputs.label: Probability mode: pretest=0.15, lr=5.2
inputs.note: pretest_odds=0.1765, posttest_odds=0.9176, posttest_prob=0.4785. Correct Fagan nomogram calculation.

assertions.note: Output format conforms to the expected structure without anomalies.

inputs.note: SKILL.md now documents that negative values must be rejected with 'Confusion matrix values must be non-negative.' Script still needs to enforce this at runtime.

assertions.text: Negative TP value rejection is documented with exact error message
assertions.note: SKILL.md Parameters section documents: 'Confusion matrix values must be non-negative.'

assertions.text: Output is valid JSON even for edge inputs
assertions.note: Output format conforms to the expected structure without anomalies.

assertions.text: Prevalence value outside 0-1 range rejection is documented
assertions.note: SKILL.md documents: 'Prevalence must be between 0 and 1.'

assertions.text: Script actually enforces negative value rejection at runtime
assertions.note: Documentation updated but script still accepts negative values; implementation gap remains

key_strengths: All three calculation modes (diagnostic, NNT, probability) produce mathematically correct results verified against EBM textbook formulas
Validation rules for negative confusion matrix values and out-of-range prevalence now documented with exact error messages
Bayes theorem PPV/NPV adjustment with prevalence is correctly implemented; references/guidelines.md cites Sackett et al.
Clean class-based design with good docstrings; 177 lines; no external dependencies

recommendations.title: Script does not enforce documented negative value validation
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md now documents that negative TP/FP/FN/TN values must be rejected, but the script still accepts them and produces mathematically impossible results like sensitivity=-1.0.
recommendations.root_cause: Documentation updated but script not re-implemented to match.
recommendations.fix: Add validation at the start of calculate(): if any of tp, fn, tn, fp < 0, raise ValueError('Confusion matrix values must be non-negative'). Also validate prevalence in [0, 1].

recommendations.title: LSP type error and unbound result variable still in script
recommendations.problem: scripts/main.py line 17 has Optional[float] vs float type error; line 166 has potentially unbound result variable. Both are documented in SKILL.md but not fixed in script.
recommendations.root_cause: SKILL.md polish documented the issues but script was not updated.
recommendations.fix: Fix line 17: change prevalence: float = None to prevalence: Optional[float] = None with from typing import Optional. Add else: result = None before json.dumps(result) at line 166.

recommendations.title: No confidence interval output for diagnostic statistics
recommendations.problem: The skill outputs point estimates for sensitivity, specificity, PPV, and NPV but no confidence intervals, which are standard in EBM reporting.
recommendations.root_cause: CI calculation not implemented in EBMCalculator.calculate().
recommendations.fix: Add Wilson score interval or Clopper-Pearson exact CI for sensitivity and specificity. Include ci_lower and ci_upper fields in the output JSON.
